strategic_objective: Investigate the origin and implications of perfect or near-perfect correlations among feature pairs.
key_checks: Determine if perfect correlations arise from inherent network traffic properties or preprocessing steps
Identify which feature pairs contribute to shortcut-like signals due to redundancy
Assess how these redundancies affect effective feature dimensionality and dependency stability
Explore if removing or consolidating redundant features alters class-conditioned patterns
success_criteria: Clear distinction between natural and preprocessing-induced redundancies
Quantification of shortcut-like risks from redundant feature pairs
Insights on how redundancy influences downstream analysis stability and interpretability
router_constraints: preserve feature pair scope for correlated features
preserve evidence refs covering correlation and redundancy (e13 to e22)
